document.title: Standard for Foods for Special Dietary Use for Persons Intolerant to Gluten
document.label: CXS 118-1979 - Standard for Foods for Special Dietary Use for Persons Intolerant to Gluten

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: 1.1 This standard applies to foods for special dietary uses that have been formulated, pr ocessed or prepared to meet the special dietary needs of people intolerant to gluten. 1.2 Foods for general consumption which by their nature are suitable for use by people with gluten intolerance may indicate such suitability in accordance with the provisions of Section 4.3.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.text: 3.1 For products referred to in 2.1.1 a) and b), the gluten content shall not exceed 20 mg/kg in the food as sold or distributed to the consumer. 3.2 For products referred to in 2.1.2 the gluten content shall not exceed 100 mg/kg in the food as sold or distributed to the consumer. 3.3 Products covered by this standard substituting important basic foods, should supply approximately the same amount of vitamins and minerals as the original foods they replace. 3.4 The products covered by this standard shall be prepared with special c are under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) to avoid contamination with gluten.

level=1
3.1 For products referred to in 2.1.1 a) and b), the gluten content shall not exceed 20 mg/kg in the food as sold or distributed to the consumer. 3.2 For products referred to in 2.1.2 the gluten content shall not exceed 100 mg/kg in the food as sold or distributed to the consumer. 3.3 Products covered by this standard substituting important basic foods, should supply approximately the same amount of vitamins and minerals as the original foods they replace. 3.4 The products covered by this standard shall be prepared with special c are under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) to avoid contamination with gluten.

page_spans.text: 3.1 For products referred to in 2.1.1 a) and b), the gluten content shall not exceed 20 mg/kg in the food as sold or distributed to the consumer. 3.2 For products referred to in 2.1.2 the gluten content shall not exceed 100 mg/kg in the food as sold or distributed to the consumer. 3.3 Products covered by this standard substituting important basic foods, should supply approximately the same amount of vitamins and minerals as the original foods they replace. 3.4 The products covered by this standard shall be prepared with special c are under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) to avoid contamination with gluten.

sections.title: Gluten-free foods
sections.text: Gluten-free foods are dietary foods a) consisting of or made only from one or more ingredients which do not contain  wheat (i.e. all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, and the gluten level does not exceed 20 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed to the consumer, and/or  b) consisting of one or more ingredients from wheat (i.e. all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT ), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, which have been specially processed to remove gluten, and the gluten level does not exceed 20 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed  to the consumer.

sections.title: Foods specially processed to reduce gluten content to a level above 20 up to 100 mg/kg
path_titles: Foods specially processed to reduce gluten content to a level above 20 up to 100 mg/kg
sections.text: These foods consist of one or more ingredients from wheat (i.e., all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT ), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, which have been specially processed to reduce the gluten content to a level above 20 up to 100 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed to the consumer .  Decisions on the marketing of products described in this section may be determined at the national level.

sections.title: Gluten
sections.text: "Gluten" is defined as a protein fraction from wheat, rye, barley, oats 1 or their crossbred varieties and derivatives thereof, insoluble in water and 0.5M NaCl. It is the part some persons are intolerant to.

sections.title: Prolamins
sections.text: Prolamins are defined as the fraction from gluten that can be extracted by 40 - 70% ethanol. Specific prolamins are gliadin from wheat, secalin from rye, hordein from barley and avenin from oats. Commonly, gluten sensitivity refers to this fraction. Prolamin content is generally taken as 50%.

Specific prolamins are gliadin from wheat, secalin from rye, hordein from barley and avenin from oats. Commonly, gluten sensitivity refers to this fraction. Prolamin content is generally taken as 50%.

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the general labelling provisions contained in the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Prepackaged Foods for Special Dietary Uses  (CXS 146-1985), and any specific labelling provisions set out in a Codex standard applying to the particular food concerned, the following provisions for the labelling of “gluten-free foods” shall apply:

sections.title: A food which, by its nature, is suitable for use as part of a gluten -free diet, shall not be designated “special dietary”, “special dietetic” or any other equivalent term. However, such a food may bear a statement on the label that “this food is by its nature gluten -free” provided that it complies with the essential composition provisions for gluten-free as set out in section 3.1 and provided that such a statement does not mislead the consumer. More detailed rules in order to ensure that the consumer is not misled may be determined at the national level.

However, such a food may bear a statement on the label that “this food is by its nature gluten -free” provided that it complies with the essential composition provisions for gluten-free as set out in section 3.1 and provided that such a statement does not mislead the consumer. More detailed rules in order to ensure that the consumer is not misled may be determined at the national level.

sections.title: General outline of the methods
sections.text:
The quantitative determination of gluten in foods and ingredients shall be based on an immunologic method or other method providing at least equal sensitivity and specificity. 
The antibody used should react with the cereal protein fractions that are toxic for persons intolerant to gluten and should not cross -react with other cereal proteins or other constituents of the foods or ingredients. 
Methods used for determination should be validated and calibrated against a certified reference material, if available. 
The detection limit has to be appropriate according to the state of the art and the technical standard. It should be 10 mg gluten/kg or below. 
The qualitative analysis that indicates the presence of gluten shall be based on relevant methods (e.g. ELISA-based methods, DNA methods).

page_spans.text:
The quantitative determination of gluten in foods and ingredients shall be based on an immunologic method or other method providing at least equal sensitivity and specificity. 
The antibody used should react with the cereal protein fractions that are toxic for persons intolerant to gluten and should not cross -react with other cereal proteins or other constituents of the foods or ingredients. 
Methods used for determination should be validated and calibrated against a certified reference material, if available. 
The detection limit has to be appropriate according to the state of the art and the technical standard. It should be 10 mg gluten/kg or below. 
The qualitative analysis that indicates the presence of gluten shall be based on relevant methods (e.g. ELISA-based methods, DNA methods).

sentences.original: Gluten-free foods are dietary foods a) consisting of or made only from one or more ingredients which do not contain  wheat (i.e. all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, and the gluten level does not exceed 20 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed to the consumer, and/or  b) consisting of one or more ingredients from wheat (i.e. all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT ), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, which have been specially processed to remove gluten, and the gluten level does not exceed 20 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed  to the consumer.

classification.sentence: Gluten-free foods are dietary foods a) consisting of or made only from one or more ingredients which do not contain  wheat (i.e. all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, and the gluten level does not exceed 20 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed to the consumer, and/or  b) consisting of one or more ingredients from wheat (i.e. all Triticum species, such as durum wheat, spelt, and khorasan wheat, which is also marketed under different trademarks such as KAMUT ), rye, barley, oats1 or their crossbred varieties, which have been specially processed to remove gluten, and the gluten level does not exceed 20 mg/kg in total, based on the food as sold or distributed  to the consumer.
classification.pattern: Subject 'Gluten-free foods' defined as dietary foods with two alternative compositions specified by connected clauses 'a)' and 'b)', each clause detailing ingredient composition and gluten level limits with clarifications

components.subject: Gluten-free foods
components.composition_clause_a: consisting of or made only from one or more ingredients which do not contain wheat (all Triticum species, rye, barley, oats or crossbred varieties) and gluten level ≤ 20 mg/kg based on food as sold/distributed
components.composition_clause_b: consisting of one or more ingredients from wheat (Triticum species), rye, barley, oats or crossbred varieties specially processed to remove gluten and gluten level ≤ 20 mg/kg based on food as sold/distributed

classification.justification: The sentence provides a detailed normative definition combining compositional and quantitative limits via a complex compound structure with enumerated alternatives; no close template match exists as it defines with embedded conditions and measurement limits under a definition communicative function.
notes: The sentence is a composite normative definition mixing obligation modality with specification of compositional and gluten content limits, a pattern not fully represented in the known templates catalog.

sentences.original: In addition to the general labelling provisions contained in the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Prepackaged Foods for Special Dietary Uses  (CXS 146-1985), and any specific labelling provisions set out in a Codex standard applying to the particular food concerned, the following provisions for the labelling of “gluten-free foods” shall apply:

classification.sentence: In addition to the general labelling provisions contained in the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Prepackaged Foods for Special Dietary Uses  (CXS 146-1985), and any specific labelling provisions set out in a Codex standard applying to the particular food concerned, the following provisions for the labelling of 2 gluten-free foods2 shall apply:
classification.pattern: [Preamble referencing related standards], [Main obligation introducing specific provisions that shall apply]

components.preamble: In addition to the general labelling provisions contained in the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods  (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Labelling of and Claims for Prepackaged Foods for Special Dietary Uses  (CXS 146-1985), and any specific labelling provisions set out in a Codex standard applying to the particular food concerned
components.main_obligation: the following provisions for the labelling of “gluten-free foods” shall apply

classification.justification: The sentence introduces an obligation to apply specific provisions while referencing multiple related standards as a preamble. This structure does not strongly match any known template but clearly serves to introduce binding normative provisions, hence an obligation modality with a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence functions as an introductory clause rather than a complete operative norm, which makes template matching difficult.

sentences.original: The quantitative determination of gluten in foods and ingredients shall be based on an immunologic method or other method providing at least equal sensitivity and specificity.

classification.sentence: Methods used for determination should be validated and calibrated against a certified reference material, if available.
classification.pattern: Subject (Methods used for determination) + Modal verb (should) + Coordinated actions (be validated and calibrated) + Condition clause (if available)
classification.justification: The sentence imposes a normative requirement with a conditional qualifier, featuring a coordinated obligation (validation and calibration) linked to an availability condition. No exact template matches this nuanced coordination and conditionality; hence it is a new pattern.
notes: The combined obligation with a conditional clause and coordinated verbs creates a structural pattern not present in standard templates.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR FOODS FOR SPECIAL DIETARY USE FOR PERSONS INTOLERANT TO GLUTEN
pages.summary: Página contém o título principal do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções estruturais ou texto interno adicional.

Página contém três seções principais numeradas (1, 2, 3) com títulos claros e subseções numeradas até o nível 3. O texto está associado corretamente a cada nó. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas nesta página. O cabeçalho 'CXS 118-1979  2' aparece no topo da página. A estrutura está consistente para uso em RAG.

A página contém duas seções principais numeradas: '4. LABELLING' e '5. METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING'. A seção 4 apresenta três subseções (4.1, 4.2, 4.3), cada uma com títulos longos baseados no texto explicativo inicial, mas com texto interno desmembrado para evitar sobreposição. A seção 4 parece continuar para a próxima página devido ao término abrupto. A seção 5 divide-se em duas subseções (5.1 e 5.2) com textos claramente delineados e completos nesta página. O cabeçalho 'CXS 118-1979  3' está presente no topo da página. Não há evidências de continuidade vinda da página anterior para as seções atuais. A estrutura atende bem ao uso em RAG, com níveis e path corretamente definidos e textos limpos, sem inclusão de cabeçalhos repetitivos.
